original_text: Microsoft AI systems are assessed using Impact Assessments. Applies to: All AI systems.
subject: Microsoft
object_data: Microsoft AI systems
condition: Applies to: All AI systems

original_text: A1.1 Assess the impact of the system on people, organizations, and society by completing an Impact Assessment early in the system's development, typically when defining the product vision and requirements.
subject: Microsoft
object_data: the impact of the system on people, organizations, and society

time_requirement.reference_event: early in the system's development, typically when defining the product vision and requirements

original_text: A1.1 Do not document the effort using the Impact Assessment template provided by the Office of Responsible AI.
subject: Microsoft
object_data: the effort
execution_style: using the Impact Assessment template provided by the Office of Responsible AI

original_text: A1.2 Review the completed Impact Assessment with the reviewers identified according to your organization's compliance process before development starts.
subject: Microsoft
object_data: the completed Impact Assessment
execution_style: with the reviewers identified according to your organization's compliance process

original_text: A1.2 ... Secure all required approvals from those reviewers.
subject: Microsoft
object_data: all required approvals from those reviewers (of the impact assessment)

original_text: A1.3 Update and review the Impact Assessment at least annually, when new intended uses are added, and before advancing to a new release stage.
subject: Microsoft
object_data: the Impact Assessment

branches.condition: when new intended uses are added
before advancing to a new release stage
branches.reference_event: advancing to a new release stage

before advancing to a new release stage

original_text: Microsoft AI systems are fit for purpose in the sense that they provide valid solutions for the problems they are designed to solve. Applies to: All AI systems.

object_data: Microsoft AI systems
execution_style: in the sense that they provide valid solutions for the problems they are designed to solve
condition: Applies to: All AI systems

original_text: A3.1 Document in the Impact Assessment how the system's use will solve the problem posed by each intended use and the specific anticipated benefits and positive outcomes for the user, recognizing that there may be multiple valid ways in which to solve the problem.

original_text: Microsoft AI systems include capabilities that support informed human oversight and control. Applies to: All AI systems.
subject: Microsoft
object_data: capabilities that support informed human oversight and control into Microsoft AI systems
condition: Applies to: All AI systems

original_text: A5.1 Identify the stakeholders who are responsible for troubleshooting, managing, operating, overseeing, and controlling the system during and after deployment.
subject: Microsoft
object_data: the stakeholders who are responsible for troubleshooting, managing, operating, overseeing, and controlling the system during and after deployment

original_text: A5.1 ... Document these stakeholders and their oversight and control responsibilities using the Impact Assessment template.
subject: Microsoft
object_data: these stakeholders and their oversight and control responsibilities

object_data: the system elements (including system UX, features, alerting and reporting functions, and educational materials) necessary for stakeholders identified in requirement A5.1 to effectively understand their oversight responsibilities and carry them out
